title: TLS, SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Encryption Boundaries · Go Semantics Case: Key Rotation Revocation Drill
summary: A service labels default encryption end-to-end, grants a broad KMS key policy, logs encryption context, and cannot rotate or revoke access without breaking unknown consumers. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the key rotation revocation drill increment in encryption and key-authorization contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.
Evaluate S3 Bucket Keys for KMS request cost while documenting encryption-context and audit changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Key Rotation Revocation Drill · Theory Key Rotation Revocation Drill
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5449 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate transport, storage, KMS authorization, context, and rotation evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary.
